title: Global News Brief: Oil, Conflicts, and Deals
summary: Today, we cover warnings about high oil prices, ongoing global conflicts, a tragic plane crash investigation, and a new international trade deal.
script:
Welcome to Your Daily Pulse. I'm your host. Today, we're looking at big news from around the world. We'll talk about money, conflicts, and new partnerships.

First up, let's talk about the economy. A very important person in finance, Larry Fink from BlackRock, has given us a warning. He says that if oil prices stay very high, like at $150 a barrel, for a long time, it will cause big problems for the world economy. He calls it a 'global recession'. This means many countries could see their economies shrink, and people might lose jobs. So, watching oil prices will be very important in the coming months. High oil prices affect everything, from how much you pay for gas to how much it costs to make and ship goods. This warning from BlackRock's boss shows us how connected the world economy is. It's a serious heads-up about what could happen if energy costs keep climbing. We need to keep an eye on this, as it could impact everyone's wallet and future.

Now, let's move to ongoing conflicts. There's a big shift in how international disputes are playing out. We've seen threats from the US against energy sites. And Iran has hit back at its neighbors in the Gulf area. Experts say this clearly shows a new and different way that countries are fighting. Old rules for international conflict seem to be changing.

Speaking of conflict, Ukraine has faced a massive attack. Russia launched nearly 950 drones at Ukraine in just one day. This is the largest attack of its kind in 24 hours. Cities all over Ukraine were hit. Local officials reported that at least eight people died, and many more were hurt. This drone attack shows the harsh reality of the conflict and the ongoing suffering for people in Ukraine. It highlights the serious dangers civilians face every day. The sheer number of drones used in this single attack is truly shocking. It means more destruction and more sorrow for those living in the affected areas.

Next, we have sad news from LaGuardia Airport. The pilots who died in a plane crash there have been named. Investigators are looking closely at what happened. They shared details from the last three minutes of talks between the cockpit and the control tower. These recordings show something very worrying. Air traffic controllers gave permission for both the plane and a fire truck to cross the same runway. This suggests there might have been a serious mix-up or error. The investigation will aim to find out exactly how this happened and and to prevent it from ever happening again. Our thoughts are with the families of the pilots and everyone affected by this tragic accident. Safety in air travel is always the top priority, and incidents like this remind us how vital clear communication and procedures are.

Finally, some positive news on the global stage. Australia and the European Union have signed a big new trade and security deal. This agreement comes after many years of talks. It's a sweeping deal that covers both trade and security between the two big partners. This is happening at a time when the world feels very uncertain. So, an agreement like this shows that countries can still work together to build stronger ties. It will likely mean more goods moving between Australia and Europe, helping businesses and people. It also shows a commitment to working together on security issues. This kind of partnership is good for stability and economic growth in a changing world. It's a positive step forward for international cooperation.

That's all for today's Your Daily Pulse. We covered warnings about the economy, tough news from conflict zones, a tragic accident, and a big new international deal. Thank you for listening. We'll be back tomorrow with more important news. Stay safe.
